description_long: This object is a long, double-edged blade with a straight profile, tapering to a sharp point. The blade appears to be made of darkened, possibly aged or weathered metal, with visible discoloration and patina suggesting extensive use or age. The handle is straight and elongated, constructed from a lighter material, likely bone or ivory, and is affixed to the blade with two prominent rivets. The handle has a simple, utilitarian design with a slight bulge at the end for grip security, and no visible guard between the blade and handle. The overall appearance is functional and somewhat rustic, with no visible decorative elements. This object could plausibly be used as a large kitchen or utility knife, or as a short sword in an urban or historical context.
description: A long, double-edged, weathered blade with a straight, light-colored handle secured by two rivets and no visible guard. The design is utilitarian and aged, with no ornamentation.
description_view_0: The full length of the blade and handle are visible, showing the double-edged blade, patina, and two rivets on the light handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the profile of the handle, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side of the blade and handle, showing similar features to view 0 but with subtle differences in patina and handle coloration.
description_view_3: Direct side view emphasizing the straightness and thickness of the handle and blade, with the blade's thin profile clearly visible.
attribute_handle_design: straight, elongated, light-colored, secured with two rivets, slight end bulge
